To determine the time period with the least amount of something per year, we consider the data provided in the table. The "live births per year," "deaths per year," and "natural change per year" are all metrics that vary across different periods. 

Looking at the data:
- The least number of live births per year occurred in the 1950-1955 period with 139,000.
- The least number of deaths per year also occurred in the 1950-1955 period with 66,000.
- The least natural change per year was in the 1950-1955 period as well, with 74,000.

Therefore, the 1950-1955 period consistently shows the lowest values across all categories per year.
